behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in shell-command segmentation. Required setup: pass a multiline command string into the segmenter/classifier input boundary and assert segment list boundaries. No OS/network/subprocess or model output is required. Higher levels were rejected because the behavior text is purely about parsing/segmentation semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the command-segmentation entrypoint with a multiline block (assignment lines + command lines) and assert it returns distinct segments per newline statement rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between segmentation and trigger classification (incident classified the whole block as `not_a_trigger`). Required setup: run the real local pipeline that segments statements then applies trigger detection. Unit was considered but rejected because the failure specifically appeared when composed logic handled the full multiline block; this is a boundary interaction, not only a single-rule parse. System was rejected because no real subprocess/HTTP is needed to classify text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a representative multiline command block with newline-separated variable assignments ending in `gh pr create` into the hook’s command-classification pipeline and assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module interaction error causing over-broad trigger matching after segmentation. Required setup: same composed segmentation+classification path as behavior 2, with a near-neighbor negative command. Unit was considered but rejected for the same reason: this guards the composed behavior that regressed in multiline handling. System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text concerns deterministic local command classification only.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same multiline structure through the full local classifier, but end with `gh issue create`; assert result is non-PR trigger and specifically not PR-create.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between trigger detection, PR-id reconstruction, and persistence adapter. Required setup: execute post-detection flow with filesystem-backed state write/read (temp path is sufficient) and verify reconstructed identifier is persisted correctly. System was considered but rejected because the behavior text does not require real `gh` execution or external API calls, only local persistence contract.
behaviors.test_description: Given a detected PR-create event payload/command context, run the hook state-update path and assert the persisted state file contains the expected reconstructed PR identifier fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: cross-module contract mismatch between writer (PostToolUse) and reader/enforcer (pre-tool hook). Required setup: write state through PostToolUse path, then load/validate it through pre-tool enforcement reader in-process with real local file I/O. System was considered but rejected because no OS-specific watcher/network/subprocess behavior is required by the behavior text; this is a local interoperability contract test. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified since no model-dependent steps exist.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger PR-create state write via post-tool logic, then invoke pre-tool enforcement read/parse/enforce path against that state file and assert enforcement activates as expected.
